items.title: Business Name
items.desc: The name of the academy or business. Used across all modules, communications, and member-facing surfaces.

items.title: Logo
items.desc: Academy logo displayed on scheduling app, invoices, marketing materials, and member portal.

items.title: Business Description
items.desc: 1–2 sentence summary of what the business does. Used by AI for context and on public-facing pages.

items.title: Contact Email
items.desc: Primary business email. Used as reply-to on automated emails and shown to members.

items.title: Phone Number
items.desc: Business phone. Shown to members and used as caller ID for SMS notifications.

items.title: Website URL
items.desc: Existing website. Used for backlinks, AI context, and auto-importing business info.

items.title: Social Media Links
items.desc: Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, etc. Used for marketing integration and public profile.

items.title: Primary Address
items.desc: Main business address. Multi-location academies add additional locations in Section 3.

items.title: Timezone
items.desc: All schedules, notifications, and billing cycles run on this timezone.

items.title: Stripe Account
items.desc: Connected Stripe account for all payments, subscriptions, and billing. Revenue flows through Stripe.

items.title: Owner Profile
items.desc: Should we collect the owner's personal name, role, and photo separately from the business profile?

items.title: Business Type
items.desc: Should we ask what type of business (academy, private coach, skills trainer, AAU program)? Could tailor the onboarding flow.

items.title: Years in Operation
items.desc: How long the business has been running. Useful for AI context and social proof. Worth collecting?

items.title: Mission Statement
items.desc: Why does this business exist? What’s the deeper purpose beyond basketball training? The AI uses this to ground conversations and content in authentic motivation.

items.title: Core Values
items.desc: 3–5 values that define how the business operates (e.g. player development over winning, positive coaching, accountability). Shapes AI tone and content direction.

items.title: Origin Story
items.desc: How and why the business was started. Powerful for marketing content, founder story ads, and AI-generated social proof.

items.title: Problem You Solve
items.desc: What specific problem do parents/athletes face that this business addresses? (e.g. “no quality training options nearby”, “kids aren’t getting enough reps”). Critical for ad copy and AI objection handling.

items.title: Target Customer Profile
items.desc: Who is the ideal customer? Age of parent, family situation, income level, what they care about. Helps AI qualify leads and tailor messaging.

items.title: Geographic Focus
items.desc: Primary area you serve and how far families typically travel. Used for lead qualification and ad targeting.

items.title: Competitive Landscape
items.desc: Who are the alternatives in your area? Other academies, rec leagues, school teams. Helps AI position against competition without naming them.

items.title: Selling Points
items.desc: 3–5 key differentiators that make the program unique. The AI weaves these naturally into conversations — one per message, not all at once.

items.title: Proof Points
items.desc: Concrete evidence that backs up differentiators: years in business, number of athletes trained, success stories, credentials. Used in ads and AI responses.

items.title: Social Proof Sources
items.desc: Where do reviews live? Google, Facebook, Instagram? Links to review pages. AI can reference and marketing can pull from these.

items.title: Testimonials
items.desc: 2–3 parent or athlete quotes the AI and marketing can use. Real names preferred but optional.

items.title: Qualification Dimensions
items.desc: Which factors matter for qualifying a lead? Location proximity, athlete age, skill level, commitment level, budget. Tells the AI what to screen for.

items.title: Ideal Lead Signals
items.desc: What does a high-intent lead look like? (e.g. asks about schedule, mentions specific child, responds quickly). Helps AI prioritize pipeline.

items.title: Disqualification Criteria
items.desc: When should the AI NOT pursue a lead? (e.g. too far away, wrong age, looking for something you don’t offer). Saves time on dead ends.

items.title: Key Messaging Angles
items.desc: Different angles to use in ad concepts: transformation story, social proof, urgency/scarcity, founder story, problem-agitation, lifestyle. Drives creative diversity for Andromeda.

items.title: Objection Map
items.desc: Common objections parents have (price, time commitment, kid is shy, bad past experience) and how to handle each. Powers AI conversation and ad copy.

items.title: Seasonal Patterns
items.desc: When are signups highest/lowest? Back to school, new year, summer? Helps AI and marketing plan campaigns around natural demand.

items.title: Brand Voice vs Tone Preference
items.desc: Should the brand voice (how content sounds) be defined here, or in the Conversation AI section? Or both — one for marketing, one for chat?

items.title: Strategy Depth at Onboarding
items.desc: How much of this should be collected upfront vs. built over time by the AI observing the business? Some of these could be AI-suggested after the first week.

items.title: Concept Library Seeding
items.desc: Should we pre-generate 8–12 ad concepts from the strategy data during onboarding? Would give the owner a head start on Andromeda-ready creative diversity.

items.title: Location Name
items.desc: Display name for this location (e.g. “Oakville Campus”, “Downtown Gym”). Used in scheduling app, booking, and member communications.

items.title: Address
items.desc: Full street address. Used for directions, Google Maps integration, and ad targeting.

items.title: Timezone
items.desc: Timezone for this specific location. Defaults to the business timezone but can differ for multi-city operations.

items.title: Location Photo
items.desc: Photo of the facility. Used in member app, booking pages, and marketing. Builds trust for first-time visitors.

items.title: Weekly Schedule
items.desc: Days and times classes run at this location. Grouped by class type or age group. Core data for booking system and AI.

items.title: Operating Hours
items.desc: General facility hours vs. class times. Are they the same or does the facility open for open gym, private training, etc.?

items.title: Holiday Schedule
items.desc: Which holidays the location is closed or has modified hours. Per-location since multi-site may differ.

items.title: Seasonal Schedule Changes
items.desc: Does the schedule change seasonally? (e.g. summer hours, March break camps). How far in advance do changes get planned?

items.title: Directions & Parking
items.desc: How to find the entrance, where to park, any access codes or buzzer info. Sent to members before first visit and used by AI.

items.title: What to Bring
items.desc: Gear athletes should bring (ball, shoes, water). Location-specific since indoor vs outdoor facilities differ.

items.title: Facility Type
items.desc: Indoor gym, outdoor court, school gym, community center, private facility. Affects logistics messaging and expectations.

items.title: Court/Space Count
items.desc: How many courts or training areas at this location. Affects scheduling capacity and concurrent class limits.

items.title: Amenities
items.desc: What’s available: water fountain, washrooms, seating for parents, pro shop, equipment rental. Good for first-visit info.

items.title: Max Capacity per Session
items.desc: Maximum athletes allowed per class at this location. Drives booking limits and waitlist triggers.

items.title: Concurrent Session Limit
items.desc: Can multiple classes run at the same time? If so, how many? Depends on court/space count.

items.title: Age Group Separation
items.desc: Are age groups trained at different times or can they overlap? Affects schedule builder logic.

items.title: Assigned Coaches
items.desc: Which coaches/staff work at this location. Links to the Staff section. A coach can work at multiple locations.

items.title: Assigned Products
items.desc: Which plans/offers are available at this location. Some products may be location-specific (e.g. outdoor-only camp).

items.title: Location-Specific Pricing
items.desc: Does pricing differ by location? (e.g. premium location charges more). Or is pricing business-wide?

items.title: Location vs. Business-Wide Data
items.desc: Which data points are truly per-location vs shared across all locations? (e.g. is the cancel policy the same everywhere or location-specific?)

items.title: Rental vs. Owned Facilities
items.desc: Does it matter if the facility is rented (school gym, community center) vs owned? Rental may mean schedule constraints, shared space, limited branding.

items.title: Virtual/Online Location
items.desc: Should we support a ‘virtual’ location for online training programs? Or is that handled differently in the Product section?

items.title: Location Contact Override
items.desc: Can a location have its own contact email/phone that differs from the business-level contact? Useful for multi-location with local managers.

items.title: Owner Name
items.desc: Full name of the business owner. Used in founder story marketing, AI context, and internal comms.

items.title: Owner Role Title
items.desc: How the owner identifies (Head Coach, Founder, Director, etc.). Shown on public-facing pages and marketing.

items.title: Owner Photo
items.desc: Photo of the owner. Used in founder story ads, about page, and member app. Builds personal connection.

items.title: Owner Bio
items.desc: Short bio — playing background, coaching philosophy, why they started. Used by AI for founder story content and ad concepts.

items.title: Owner Email
items.desc: Personal/work email for the owner. Used for account login, escalation notifications, and billing alerts.

items.title: Coach Name
items.desc: Full name. Shown in scheduling app, class detail view, and member communications.

items.title: Coach Photo
items.desc: Profile photo. Shown in class detail (APP-017) and coach spotlight content. Parents want to see who’s coaching their kids.

items.title: Coach Bio
items.desc: Short bio: playing experience, certifications, coaching style. Used in scheduling app and AI-generated coach spotlight posts.

items.title: Credentials & Certifications
items.desc: Formal qualifications: coaching certs, playing history (college/pro), years of experience. AI uses this for trust-building with leads.

items.title: Coaching Specialty
items.desc: What this coach is known for (e.g. ball handling, shooting, defense, youth development). Helps with class assignments and marketing.

items.title: Coach Email / Phone
items.desc: Contact info for internal notifications (session reminders, cancellations, new bookings). Not shown to members.

items.title: Assigned Locations
items.desc: Which locations this coach works at. Links to Section 3. A coach can work at multiple locations.

items.title: Coach Ratio
items.desc: Number of coaches per session or group (e.g. “2 coaches per session”). Used by AI to answer parent questions about supervision.

items.title: Group Sizes
items.desc: Typical number of athletes per group. Combined with coach ratio, this tells the story of attention per athlete.

items.title: Skill Levels Coached
items.desc: What skill levels the program accepts (all levels, beginner-only, advanced, etc.). Per-coach or business-wide.

items.title: Staff Roles & Permissions
items.desc: Beyond coaches — are there admin staff, assistant coaches, or front desk? What level of access does each role get in BAM OS?

items.title: Coach Availability
items.desc: Which days/times each coach is available. Used by schedule builder to prevent conflicts and enable substitutions.

items.title: Substitute Coach Rules
items.desc: What happens when a coach can’t make a session? Auto-assign a sub, cancel, or notify owner? Affects scheduling logic.

items.title: Coach Onboarding
items.desc: Do coaches create their own profiles or does the owner fill it in? If coaches self-serve, they need login access and a profile builder.

items.title: Coach Pay / Compensation
items.desc: Should BAM OS track coach pay rates (per session, hourly, monthly)? Could enable payroll reports. Or is this out of scope?

items.title: Coach Performance Metrics
items.desc: Should we track attendance rates, member feedback, or retention per coach? Useful for owner but may be V2+ scope.

items.title: Public vs Private Profile Data
items.desc: Which coach data is shown to members (name, photo, bio) vs internal only (email, phone, pay rate)? Need clear visibility rules.

items.title: Business Description
items.desc: 1–2 sentence summary of what the business does and what services they offer. Moved here from Identity — belongs with the product definition.

items.title: Sport / Activity Type
items.desc: What sport or activity (basketball, soccer, multi-sport, fitness). Determines terminology and UI throughout the platform.

items.title: Service Types Offered
items.desc: What do you offer? Group classes, private training, camps, clinics, online programs, open gym. Defines product catalog structure.

items.title: Age Range
items.desc: Minimum and maximum age of athletes accepted. Determines product eligibility and lead qualification.

items.title: Skill Levels
items.desc: What skill levels the program accepts (all levels, beginner, intermediate, advanced). Moved from Staff — this is about product eligibility.

items.title: Training Offer Definition
items.desc: The core offer: what does the athlete get? Target age, skill level, session format (group/private/both), session duration, frequency. This is the “what” of the product. (PRD-001)

items.title: Offer Name & Description
items.desc: Human-readable name and description for each offer. Shown to members in scheduling app and on booking pages. Can be AI-suggested from the offer details.

items.title: Offer Inclusions
items.desc: What’s included beyond training: video review, custom programming, WhatsApp access, merchandise, etc. Differentiates tiers.

items.title: Location Assignment
items.desc: Which locations offer this product. Some offers may be location-specific (e.g. outdoor camp only at park location).

items.title: Pricing Model
items.desc: How is this offer priced? Monthly subscription, per-session, credit pack, or flat fee. Determines Stripe setup.

items.title: Subscription Plans
items.desc: For recurring billing: plan name, cadence (weekly/monthly), credits per cycle, commitment length, post-commitment behavior. (PRD-002)

items.title: Pricing Tiers
items.desc: Different price points for the same offer (e.g. 1x/week $200, 2x/week $280, unlimited $565). The full pricing table.

items.title: Trial Period
items.desc: Free or discounted trial before first charge. Duration in days, what’s included, auto-charge at end. Per-plan configuration.

items.title: Session Packages
items.desc: Pre-paid credit bundles (e.g. 10-pack) with set expiry. Alternative to subscription for drop-in athletes. (PRD-006)

items.title: Pay-Per-Session
items.desc: Single-session drop-in pricing with optional expiry. Simplest pricing model. (PRD-007)

items.title: Pricing Transparency Mode
items.desc: How the AI handles pricing questions: show exact prices, show a range, or hide until after trial. Business-wide setting.

items.title: Sibling Discount
items.desc: Discount for families with multiple athletes. Flat amount, percentage, or custom (e.g. “50% off each additional sibling”).

items.title: Referral Discount
items.desc: What existing members get for referring new athletes. Free month, credit, percentage off. Drives word-of-mouth.

items.title: Prepayment Discounts
items.desc: Discounts for 3-month, 6-month, or annual prepayment. Can be auto-imported from Stripe if plans already exist.

items.title: Promo Codes
items.desc: One-time discount codes: type (percentage/flat), amount, expiry date, usage limits. Used in campaigns and partner deals.

items.title: Seasonal Promotions
items.desc: Time-limited pricing events (back to school, new year, summer). When are signups highest/lowest? Moved from Strategy.

items.title: Coach Ratio per Class
items.desc: Number of coaches per session type. Moved from Staff. Defined per product/class type, not per coach.

items.title: Group Size Limits
items.desc: Max athletes per class type. Moved from Staff. Tied to the product definition, not the location.

items.title: Session Duration
items.desc: How long each class/session lasts. May vary by product type (group = 60min, private = 45min).

items.title: Private Training Availability
items.desc: Whether 1-on-1 private sessions are available and any restrictions (members only, separate pricing, specific coaches).

items.title: Holiday Schedule Impact
items.desc: Does the holiday schedule affect billing? (e.g. no class on holidays but still charged, or prorated). Moved from Locations — this is a product/billing decision.

items.title: Camp / Special Programs
items.desc: One-off or seasonal programs (summer camp, March break, holiday clinics). Different from recurring subscriptions — separate product type.

items.title: Offer vs Plan vs Product
items.desc: What’s the hierarchy? Recommendation: an Offer is what the athlete gets (training definition). A Plan is how they pay (subscription/pack/drop-in). A Product ties an Offer to a Plan at a Location.

items.title: Credit System
items.desc: Should all plans use credits (book X sessions per cycle) or should unlimited plans bypass credits entirely? Affects booking logic.

items.title: Overage Handling
items.desc: What happens when a member uses all their credits but wants to book more? Block, charge per-session, or allow with warning?

items.title: Plan Migration
items.desc: If an existing academy has members on plans in Stripe already, can we import those? Or do they need to recreate everything?

items.title: Makeup Credits
items.desc: When a member misses a session, do they get a makeup credit? How long does it last? Is this a policy or product setting?

items.title: Cancellation Notice Period
items.desc: How much notice is required to cancel a membership? (e.g. 30 days, end of billing cycle, immediate). Determines when billing stops.

items.title: Cancellation Flow
items.desc: What happens when a member cancels? Coach-mediated (retention attempt) or self-serve? BAM OS currently specs coach-mediated (MEM-006).

items.title: Early Termination Fee
items.desc: If the member is in a commitment period, is there a fee to cancel early? Amount or formula. Some academies don’t charge this.

items.title: Cancellation Reason Collection
items.desc: Should the system ask why they’re cancelling? (moving, cost, schedule, unhappy, other). Useful for retention insights.

items.title: Pause Allowed
items.desc: Can members pause their membership? Yes/No. If yes, all fields below apply.

items.title: Max Pause Duration
items.desc: Maximum length of a single pause (e.g. 4 weeks, 8 weeks). Prevents indefinite freezes.

items.title: Pauses Per Year
items.desc: How many times can a member pause per year? (e.g. 1, 2, unlimited). Prevents abuse.

items.title: Minimum Membership Before Pause
items.desc: Must the member be active for a minimum period before first pause? (e.g. 30 days, 60 days, none).

items.title: Pause Billing Behavior
items.desc: What happens to billing during pause? Fully stopped, reduced rate, or credits frozen? Most academies fully stop.

items.title: Payment Methods Accepted
items.desc: Which payment methods: credit card, debit, ACH, e-transfer, cash. Stripe handles card/ACH; cash/e-transfer need manual tracking.

items.title: Failed Payment Retry Schedule
items.desc: How many times and how often to retry a failed payment before suspension. (e.g. retry at 3, 5, 7 days then suspend). SET-004 dunning config.

items.title: Grace Period After Failed Payment
items.desc: How long after a failed payment before the member loses access? Immediate, 3 days, 7 days? Affects member experience.

items.title: Suspension vs Cancellation
items.desc: After dunning fails, is the member suspended (can reactivate by paying) or cancelled (must re-signup)? Most use suspension.

items.title: Booking Cancellation Window
items.desc: How far in advance must a member cancel a booked class? (e.g. 12 hours, 24 hours). Within window = credit back. Outside = late cancel.

items.title: Late Cancel Consequence
items.desc: What happens on a late cancel? Credit lost, small fee, warning, or nothing? Drives attendance accountability.

items.title: No-Show Policy
items.desc: What happens when a member books but doesn’t show? Same as late cancel, or stricter? Some academies flag repeat offenders.

items.title: Waitlist Policy
items.desc: When a class is full, can members join a waitlist? How long do they have to accept when a spot opens? (e.g. 1 hour window).

items.title: Advance Booking Limit
items.desc: How far in advance can members book? (e.g. 1 week, 2 weeks, rolling month). Prevents hoarding of popular time slots.

items.title: Policy Flexibility Areas
items.desc: Where can staff bend the rules? (e.g. offer a second free trial, waive a late cancel, extend a pause). Defines AI and staff discretion boundaries.

items.title: Refund Policy
items.desc: Under what conditions are refunds given? Never, within X days, case-by-case, prorated? Affects Stripe refund workflow.

items.title: Transfer Policy
items.desc: Can a member transfer their membership to another person? Some academies allow it, most don’t.

items.title: Liability Waiver Required
items.desc: Must members sign a liability waiver? Almost always yes for sports. Template is platform-provided, academy customizes.

items.title: Terms of Service
items.desc: Platform-provided ToS that members agree to during sign-up. Covers membership rules, billing, and dispute resolution.

items.title: Media Release
items.desc: Permission to use photos/videos of the athlete in marketing. Required for content generation and social media.

items.title: Under-18 Consent
items.desc: Parent/guardian must sign for minors. Built into the member onboarding flow (APP-005) since parents create accounts.

items.title: Data Retention / Deletion
items.desc: How long is member data kept after cancellation? 90-day soft delete per current spec (APP-023c). GDPR/CCPA compliance.

items.title: Policy Presets
items.desc: Should we offer preset policy templates (e.g. “Flexible”, “Standard”, “Strict”) that pre-fill all the fields above? Reduces onboarding friction.

items.title: Policy Display to Members
items.desc: Should policies be shown in the member app (transparent) or only enforced silently? Most modern platforms show them.

items.title: AI Policy Enforcement
items.desc: Should the AI automatically enforce policies (e.g. auto-reject late cancels) or always defer to the owner? Ties to the three-tier change management model.

items.title: Tone Preference
items.desc: How casual or professional should the bot sound? Options: Professional, Friendly/Casual, High-Energy/Hype, Motivational Coach. Can differ between marketing content and lead conversations.

items.title: Communication Style
items.desc: Short and punchy or detailed and informative? Should the bot use emojis? Exclamation marks? How long should typical responses be?

items.title: Brand Voice Consistency
items.desc: Should the AI voice match the owner’s natural speaking style? If so, the LLM chat in Strategy helps calibrate this from the owner’s own words.

items.title: Do Not Say List
items.desc: Things the AI should never say. Owner can always add to this. Examples: competitor names, specific pricing before trial, promises about results, medical claims.

items.title: Topics to Escalate
items.desc: Questions or situations the AI should hand off to the owner instead of answering. Examples: injury questions, refund disputes, complaints about a coach.

items.title: Competitor Mention Rules
items.desc: How should the AI handle when a lead mentions a competitor? Never name them, acknowledge and redirect, or compare directly?

items.title: Pricing Discussion Rules
items.desc: When can the AI discuss pricing? Ties to Pricing Transparency Mode (business-level). The AI needs to know: share range, share exact, or redirect to trial first.

items.title: Trial Booking Link
items.desc: The URL where leads book their free trial. The AI’s primary conversion action — every conversation should move toward this.

items.title: Qualification Flow
items.desc: In what order should the AI qualify leads? Moved from Strategy. Recommended: interest → age → location → schedule fit → book trial.

items.title: Objection Handling
items.desc: How should the AI handle common objections? Price, time commitment, shy kid, bad past experience, “let me think about it.” Moved from Strategy — this is Sales section input displayed as a selectable list.

items.title: Selling Point Delivery
items.desc: The AI weaves selling points naturally — one per message, not all at once. Should it prioritize certain points based on lead signals?

items.title: Ghosting Strategy
items.desc: What happens when a lead stops responding? AUTO_SEQUENCE (bot follows up automatically) or NOTIFY_OWNER (owner gets alerted to call). Default: AUTO_SEQUENCE.

items.title: Follow-Up Cadence
items.desc: How often and how many times the bot follows up: next day, then 2 days, then close. Or custom intervals. Default provided.

items.title: Re-Engagement Rules
items.desc: Should the AI re-engage cold leads after X days/weeks? What message? Some academies do seasonal re-engagement campaigns.

items.title: Conversation Review
items.desc: Can the owner review AI conversations and flag bad responses? This trains the AI over time and feeds into the Do Not Say list.

items.title: Owner Corrections
items.desc: When the owner corrects the AI (e.g. “don’t say that” or “say this instead”), should corrections apply globally or per-conversation?

items.title: Performance Metrics
items.desc: What should the owner see about AI performance? Response rate, booking rate, average response time, lead-to-trial conversion?

items.title: AI for Members vs Leads
items.desc: Is the conversation AI only for leads (booking agent) or also for existing members (support, rebooking, upselling)? Current spec is leads-only.

items.title: Multi-Channel
items.desc: Does the AI respond on SMS, Instagram DM, Facebook Messenger, WhatsApp, or just one channel? Affects integration scope.

items.title: Human Handoff UX
items.desc: When the AI escalates to the owner, what does that look like? Does the owner take over the same thread or start a new one?

items.title: AI Personality Name
items.desc: Should the AI have a name? BAM OS uses “Sage” internally. Should the lead-facing bot also be Sage, or should it use the academy’s name?

items.title: Stripe Account
items.desc: Connect Stripe for all payments, subscriptions, and billing. Moved from Business Info. OAuth connect flow.

items.title: Stripe Plan Import
items.desc: If the academy already has plans in Stripe, import them into BAM OS instead of recreating. Saves onboarding time.

items.title: GoHighLevel Account
items.desc: Connect existing GHL account. For MVP, BAM OS integrates with GHL for CRM, pipeline, and automation workflows.

items.title: Existing Member Data Import
items.desc: Does the academy have an existing member list (spreadsheet, other platform)? Import members to avoid re-signup.

items.title: Existing Lead Data Import
items.desc: Import existing leads/prospects from a CRM or spreadsheet. Pre-populates the sales pipeline.

items.title: Meta Business Manager
items.desc: Connect Meta for ad management. Required for marketing module. Includes Pixel and Conversions API setup. (SET-010)

items.title: Google Business Profile
items.desc: Connect Google Business for review management, local SEO, and business info sync.

items.title: Social Proof Sources
items.desc: Where do reviews live? Google, Facebook, Yelp, Instagram. Links to review pages for AI and marketing to pull from. Moved from Strategy.

items.title: SMS Provider
items.desc: SMS sending for notifications, reminders, and AI conversations. Platform-provided or BYO Twilio account.

items.title: Email Provider
items.desc: Email sending for automated emails, marketing, and transactional messages. Platform-provided or custom SMTP.

items.title: AI Conversation Channels
items.desc: Which channels should the AI respond on? SMS, Instagram DM, Facebook Messenger, WhatsApp, website chat. Owner selects from connected integrations.

items.title: Notification Preferences
items.desc: Default notification settings: which events trigger SMS vs email vs push. Owner configures, members can override. (SET-007)

items.title: Onboarding Links
items.desc: Generate trackable member sign-up links per plan and location. Coach sends these to new members. If scheduling app exists, this is the app download link. (SET-005)

items.title: Test Member Signup
items.desc: Owner runs through the member onboarding flow (APP-005) as a test before going live. Validates everything works end-to-end.

items.title: First Class Setup
items.desc: At least one class/session must be created in the schedule before members can book. Validates the schedule builder works.

items.title: Welcome Message Template
items.desc: What automated message goes to the first member who signs up? Sets the tone. Can be AI-drafted from strategy data.

items.title: Website Builder
items.desc: BAM OS goal is to make the website for the academy. Is this part of onboarding or a separate module? What data does it need beyond what we’ve already collected?

items.title: Domain & Hosting
items.desc: Does BAM OS provide hosting? Custom domain support? Or just a subdomain (academy.fullcontrol.app)?

items.title: Go-Live Validation
items.desc: Should there be a system-level “readiness check” that validates all required data is in before the academy can go live? Prevents incomplete setups.

items.title: Migration Support
items.desc: For academies switching from another platform (Mindbody, Zen Planner, TeamSnap), what migration tooling or support do we need?

items.title: Class/Session Creation
items.desc: The core unit: a recurring time slot with a name, day, time, duration, location, coach, age group, and capacity. This is how the owner builds their weekly schedule.

items.title: Session Duration
items.desc: How long each class lasts. May vary by type (group = 60min, private = 45min). Set per class, not globally. Moved from Product.

items.title: Group Size / Capacity per Session
items.desc: Max athletes per class. Set per session in the schedule builder. This IS the capacity — no separate field needed. Moved from Locations and Product.

items.title: Coach Assignment per Session
items.desc: Which coach runs each session. Pulled from the Staff section. Enables the scheduling app to show coach name/photo.

items.title: Coach Ratio per Session
items.desc: Number of coaches per class type (e.g. 2 coaches for a group of 12). Moved from Staff. Set per class type in the builder.

items.title: Age Group Assignment
items.desc: Which age group this session serves. Determines eligibility filtering in the member booking flow.

items.title: Skill Level Assignment
items.desc: Which skill level this session targets (if different from business-wide). Some sessions may be advanced-only.

items.title: Recurring vs One-Off
items.desc: Is this a weekly recurring session or a one-time event (camp day, clinic, makeup class)?

items.title: Location Assignment
items.desc: Which location this session runs at. Pre-filtered from Location section. Required for multi-location academies.

items.title: Coach Available Days/Times
items.desc: Which days and times each coach is available to work. Prevents schedule conflicts. Moved from Staff.

items.title: Substitute Coach Rules
items.desc: What happens when a coach can’t make it? Auto-assign sub, cancel session, or notify owner. Moved from Staff.

items.title: Coach Conflict Detection
items.desc: Should the system warn if a coach is double-booked across locations or overlapping sessions?

items.title: Credit-Based Booking
items.desc: Do members use credits to book sessions? Each plan grants X credits per cycle. Booking a class costs 1+ credits. Moved from Product.

items.title: Unlimited Plans
items.desc: Do unlimited plans bypass the credit system entirely, or do they get a very high credit count? Affects booking logic.

items.title: Credit Rollover
items.desc: Do unused credits roll over to the next billing cycle, or expire? Most academies don’t roll over.

items.title: Credit Cost per Session Type
items.desc: Do different session types cost different credits? (e.g. group = 1 credit, private = 3 credits). Or flat 1 credit per session?

items.title: Booking Cancellation Window
items.desc: How far in advance must a member cancel a booked class? (e.g. 12h, 24h). Within window = credit back. Outside = late cancel. Moved from Policy.

items.title: Late Cancel Consequence
items.desc: What happens on a late cancel? Credit lost, small fee, warning, or nothing. Moved from Policy.

items.title: No-Show Policy
items.desc: What happens when a member doesn’t show up? Same as late cancel or stricter? Flag repeat offenders? Moved from Policy.

items.title: Waitlist Behavior
items.desc: When a class is full, can members join a waitlist? How long to accept a spot when one opens? (e.g. 1h window). Moved from Policy.

items.title: Advance Booking Limit
items.desc: How far ahead can members book? (1 week, 2 weeks, rolling month). Prevents hoarding popular slots. Moved from Policy.

items.title: Same-Day Booking
items.desc: Can members book a class on the same day? If so, how close to start time? (e.g. up to 1 hour before).

items.title: Check-In Method
items.desc: How do members check in? QR code scan, kiosk mode (tablet), in-app button, or coach marks attendance manually? (APP-030)

items.title: Attendance Tracking
items.desc: Is attendance automatically tracked when a member checks in? Does it update the member’s attendance history and streak? (MEM-001)

items.title: Walk-In Handling
items.desc: Can someone show up without booking? If so, are they flagged as “unbooked” in the roster? Do they use a credit?

items.title: Member Schedule View
items.desc: How do members see the schedule? List view (grouped by day), calendar view, or both? (APP-015a/b)

items.title: Eligibility Filtering
items.desc: Members only see classes they’re eligible for (by age, skill, plan). Ineligible classes hidden or shown as locked.

items.title: Real-Time Capacity Display
items.desc: Show available spots in real time (e.g. “3 spots left”). Color codes: green (>50%), yellow (1-50%), red (full).

items.title: Recurring Booking
items.desc: Can members book the same class every week automatically (recurring booking)? Or must they book each week individually?

items.title: Class Cancellation by Coach
items.desc: What happens when a coach cancels a session? All members notified, credits refunded, waitlist cleared. How much notice required?

items.title: Schedule Conflicts
items.desc: Can a member book two classes at the same time? Probably no — but what about siblings on the same account?

items.title: Schedule Template vs Manual
items.desc: Should the owner build a weekly template that auto-generates sessions, or create each week’s sessions manually? Template is more scalable.

items.title: Session Notes
items.desc: Can the owner/coach add notes to a session (e.g. “Focus on defense today”, “Bring basketball”)? Shown to members in booking detail.

items.title: Qualification Dimensions
items.desc: Which factors matter for qualifying a lead: location proximity, athlete age, skill level, commitment level, budget. Tells the AI what to screen for. Moved from Strategy.

items.title: Qualification Priority Order
items.desc: In what order should the AI qualify? Recommended: interest → age → location → schedule fit → book trial. Owner can reorder.

items.title: Disqualification Logic
items.desc: Anything outside the qualification dimensions = auto-disqualified. No separate config needed — derived from dimensions.

items.title: Common Objections List
items.desc: Preset list of objections parents typically have. Owner checks which ones apply and can add custom ones. Moved from Strategy.

items.title: Objection Responses
items.desc: For each checked objection, a recommended response the AI uses. AI-suggested but owner can customize. Selectable list UI.

items.title: Pipeline Stages
items.desc: What stages does a lead go through? Default: New → Contacted → Qualified → Trial Booked → Trial Attended → Enrolled → Lost. Owner can customize.

items.title: Lead Source Tracking
items.desc: Where do leads come from? Instagram, Facebook, Google, referral, walk-in, website, other. Tracked per lead for ROI analysis.

items.title: Auto-Stage Movement
items.desc: Should the AI automatically move leads between stages based on actions? (e.g. trial booked → auto-move to Trial Booked stage).

items.title: Lead Assignment Rules
items.desc: For multi-coach academies: are leads assigned to a specific coach or handled by whoever responds first?

items.title: Trial Booking Link
items.desc: URL where leads book their free trial. Already in Conversation AI — listed here as the sales conversion endpoint.

items.title: Pre-Trial Communication
items.desc: What automated messages go out before the trial? Confirmation, reminder, what to bring, directions. Configurable sequence.

items.title: Post-Trial Follow-Up
items.desc: What happens after the trial? Automated message sequence: thank you → how was it → enrollment offer → follow-up if no response.

items.title: Trial-to-Enrollment Conversion Flow
items.desc: How does a trial member become a paying member? In-app enrollment, coach closes, or AI sends signup link?

items.title: Lead Capture Form Fields
items.desc: What info do you collect when a lead first reaches out? Name, email, phone, athlete age, how they heard about you. Configurable.

items.title: Form Placement
items.desc: Where does the lead capture form live? Website, landing page, Instagram link-in-bio, QR code at events. Multiple placements possible.

items.title: Auto-Response on Form Submit
items.desc: What happens immediately when someone fills out the form? AI starts a conversation, sends a booking link, or just confirms receipt.

items.title: Sales Reporting
items.desc: What sales metrics does the owner need? Trial conversion rate, lead source ROI, pipeline velocity, AI vs human close rate. Feature, not onboarding data.

items.title: Manual Lead Entry
items.desc: Can the owner manually add a lead (e.g. from a phone call or walk-in)? Minimum fields: name + phone or email.

items.title: Lost Lead Reasons
items.desc: When a lead is marked as lost, should the system ask why? (too expensive, wrong location, chose competitor, no response). Useful for pattern analysis.
